hypothesis: If 3D molecule generators are retrained on Kekulized, chemically accurate valency representations rather than original valence data, then the generated molecules will show closer-to-optimized geometries with smaller bond-length differences (Δr), bond-angle differences (Δθ), torsion-angle differences (Δφ), and lower relaxation energy (ΔE_relax) under GFN2-xTB evaluation than generators trained on original data, demonstrating that chemically accurate valency handling improves geometric realism.
